revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:池田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.826056|N|135.434056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) |Belonging = |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|en|Ikeda, Osaka|qid=Q817965|lt=Ikeda|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}{{ill|Satsukigaoka|ja|五月丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsukigaoka|1=Satsukigaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372450}} 1-chome 6-15 (within Chausuyama Park) |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 34.12 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 26|coordinates = 2.75 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (Karakagami style) |map_size = Mound length: 59.5m |built = Mid {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (1) |Deceased = |excavations = Grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments |Historic Site = Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = excavations (Ikeda City Designated Tangible designation1) |notes = {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|qid=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun|1=Mesuriyama Kofun}} (in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}) is a 1/4 map_size replica
Same type as {{ill|Shiramizu Hyōzuka Kofun|ja|白水瓢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramizu Hyōzuka Kofun|1=Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97173015}} (in {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}) |map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=49|coordinates=34.12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=26|lon_sec=2.75|map_dot_label= Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-Southeast.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|34.12|N|135|26|2.75|E}}}} }} [[File:Ikeda Chausuyama.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{center|Aerial view of Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] '''Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun''' (池田茶臼山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|en|Ikeda, Osaka|qid=Q817965|lt=Ikeda|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}, {{ill|Satsukigaoka|ja|五月丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsukigaoka|1=Satsukigaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372450}}. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus and is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Osaka Prefecture. The burial goods are Historic Site1 as tangible cultural property by Ikeda City. == Overview == Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun is located in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, constructed at the saddle point of the Satsukiyama hills{{Sfn|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It suffered from looting in the past and was slated for demolition during the Satsukigaoka land redevelopment project of 1957 (Showa 32). However, preservation efforts led to its excavation in {{ill|1958|en|1958|qid=Q5253|lt=1958|1=1958}} (Showa 33) and subsequent protection{{Sfn|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is a "karakagami-style" keyhole-shaped tumulus with a less developed front section[https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASJ9P533HJ9PPPTB005.html "Ikeda Chausuyama is a Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Site Explanation Meeting on the 24th"] (Asahi Shimbun, September 22, 2016).. The front section faces southeast{{Sfn|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound's exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones) and haniwa rows. The burial facility is a pit-style stone chamber at the center of the rear circular section{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Although the chamber was looted, several grave goods were discovered during excavation{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Additionally, haniwa cylindrical coffins were found near the boundary of the front and rear sections and at the north side of the rear section{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. It is considered one of the oldest chief tombs constructed on the left bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagawa River|1=Ina River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11571867}}. The nearby {{ill|Gosandō Kofun|ja|娯三堂古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosandō Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ikeda City, Ayaha; circular mound, diameter 27m) is believed to be a successor but shows a significant size reduction, indicating the decline of regional power along the Inagawa River. The site was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Historic Site in {{ill|1972|en|1972|qid=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}} (Showa 47), and the burial goods were Historic Site1 as Ikeda City Tangible designation1 in {{ill|1978|en|1978|qid=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53). Today, the site is maintained as Chausuyama Park, a historic site park open to the public. == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|1958|en|1958|qid=Q5253|lt=1958|1=1958}} (Showa 33): Excavation{{Sfn|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * {{ill|1972|en|1972|qid=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}} (Showa 47), March 31: Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Historic Site. * {{ill|1978|en|1978|qid=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53), October 31: excavations Historic Site1 as Ikeda City Tangible designation1. * {{ill|2015|en|2015|qid=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}}–{{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}} (Heisei 27–28): Excavation by Ikeda City Education Committee. * {{ill|2017|en|2017|qid=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}} (Heisei 29): Conservation work completed[http://www.city.ikeda.osaka.jp/event/1439943604318.html Osaka Prefecture Historic Site Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun Conservation Work Completed] (Ikeda City Homepage).. == Mound == The mound area are as follows: * Mound length: approximately 59.5 meters * Rear circular section: diameter 34 meters * Front rectangular section: width 18 meters The mound is a 1/4 map_size replica of {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|qid=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun|1=Mesuriyama Kofun}} (Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture). {{ill|Shiramizu Hyōzuka Kofun|ja|白水瓢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramizu Hyōzuka Kofun|1=Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97173015}} (Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture) is also a 1/4 map_size replica of Mesuriyama Kofun and is nearly identical to Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun. File:Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear circular section File:Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section File:Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Mound (pre-restoration)
{{small|Left: front section, Right: rear circular section.}} File:Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front section (pre-restoration) == burial == [[File:Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun, funcho-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular section top}}]] Three burial facilities are recognized: one pit-style stone chamber and two haniwa cylindrical coffins. ; Pit-Style Stone Chamber : Located at the center of the rear circular section. The chamber's axis is perpendicular to the mound's axis, with a total length of 6.35 meters. The floor is a clay coffin bed, and the inner walls were painted with red pigment. Though looted, excavations yielded grave goods such as pedestal-shaped {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} (described below){{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. ; Haniwa Cylindrical Coffins : Located at the boundary of the rear and front sections. Excavations yielded one small knife{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. ; Haniwa Cylindrical Coffin : Located at the base of the rear circular section's north side{{Sfn|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. File:Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear circular section top (pre-restoration) == burial goods == [[File:池田茶臼山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|249x249px|{{center|Grave goods}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Ikeda City Historical Folk Museum|ja|池田市立歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda City Historical Folk Museum|1=Ikeda City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11551839}}.}}]] [[File:池田茶臼山古墳出土 円筒埴輪棺・脚付埦形土器.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Haniwa cylindrical coffin}}]] The following grave goods were discovered during the excavation of the pit-style stone chamber: * One pedestal-shaped {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} * One jasper stone bracelet * Six jasper beads * Glass beads * Iron burial goods File:池田茶臼山古墳出土 脚付埦形土器.JPG|Pedestal-shaped Haji pottery == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun - Historic Site1 on March 31, 1972[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Homepage).[https://www.city.ikeda.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka/bunka/13167.html List of Designated Historic Site] (Ikeda City Homepage).. === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1: ''' excavations from Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - preserved at {{ill|Ikeda City Historical Folk Museum|ja|池田市立歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda City Historical Folk Museum|1=Ikeda City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11551839}}. Designated on October 31, 1978. == Local Intypeation == '''location''' * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|en|Ikeda, Osaka|qid=Q817965|lt=Ikeda|1=Ikeda, Osaka}} Satsukigaoka 1-chome '''Access''' * Bus: From {{ill|Ikeda Station|en|Ikeda Station|qid=Q1206276|lt=Ikeda Station (Osaka)|1=Ikeda Station}} ({{ill|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|en|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|qid=Q3832859|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}}), take a {{ill|Hankyu Bus|ja|阪急バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Bus|1=Hankyu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11656745}} to "Satsukigaoka Elementary School" bus stop, then walk about 5 minutes. * Alternatively, from {{ill|Ishibashi handai-mae Station|en|Ishibashi handai-mae Station|qid=Q866078|lt=Ishibashi Handai-mae Station|1=Ishibashi handai-mae Station}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line and {{ill|Hankyū Minoo Line|en|Hankyū Minoo Line|qid=Q246334|lt=Hankyu Minoo Line|1=Hankyū Minoo Line}}), take a Hankyu Bus to "Satsukigaoka 1-chome" bus stop, right next to the site. '''Related Facilities''' * {{ill|Ikeda City Historical Folk Museum|ja|池田市立歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda City Historical Folk Museum|1=Ikeda City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11551839}} (Satsukigaoka, Ikeda City): About a 5-minute walk southwest; preserves and exhibits burial goods from Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun. == notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Intypeation board * {{Cite book|title=Research on Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun (Ikeda City Historic Site Survey Report, Volume 1)|publisher=Ikeda City|year=1970|ref={{Harvid|Research on Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun|1970}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 28 - Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda City)|ja|鉢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda City)|1=Hachizuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18989641}} * {{ill|Satsukigaoka Kofun|ja|五月丘古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsukigaoka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五月ヶ丘古墳}} * {{ill|Shiramizu Hyōzuka Kofun|ja|白水瓢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramizu Hyōzuka Kofun|1=Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97173015}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|池田茶臼山古墳|version=99995432|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:池田茶臼山古墳]] {{Commons category|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11552118}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
